month it crashed in two months and then

crashed in the summer recovered the
highs by November by the end of mid

the year.

this is the end of presentation. I've

that is a very reasonable expectation if
President Trump and and and Secretaries
Besset and Lutnik are actually intending
to go through alongside Elon Musk with

through that short-term pain for the
long-term gain of you know kind of
ringing the the the excesses of the you
know the the fiscal large s Biden
economy out of the system that you know
again going from scenario A paradigm A
to paradigm B will you have to go in the
valley you can't just jump from one
mountain top to the other mountain top.
The other mountain top's 100 miles away,
even though it looks like you can throw
a rock at it. You have to go through the
valley and to get back to the top. And
and that's exactly what the 1998 analog
uh uh uh suggests. Uh we could
potentially see here if things get bad.
Things may not get bad, by the way. They
may not be trying to kid us the economy.
You know, maybe I'm reading too much
into it. Maybe they don't sink the
economy. Maybe the Fed just control
prints anyway. And maybe the China
stepping on the gas and we're all to the
races. But guess what? You don't have to
live in fear of not knowing the outcome.
just trust KISS and Dr. Mo will get us
into the proper allocations from a
medium-term perspective. Is it going to
sell the high or buy the bottom?
Absolutely not. If that's your
expectation, you will be severely
disappointed. But if your goal is to
stack a a series of high probability
bets on top of each other for a long
period of time so that you retire on
time and comfortably, then KISS and Dr.
Mo are the risk management overlays for
you. So, we'll wrap it up there. Uh
thank you. We'll catch you back here
next month. Cheers. And so lastly, we
view 1998 as the best analog as the best
market analog where President Trump
kitchen sinks the US economy and
ultimately forces the Fed to print. Um,
so what happened in 1998, we had a very
swift crash in the market. You know, we
had a retest of the lows a couple of
months later and then we was off to the
races. You know, we recovered the highs
within three months and we were up, you
know, 25% or 28 up 28% from the lows of
of of August 31st through the end of the
year. But again, we had to crash to get
to those lows. And so I continue to
think this is the most likely scenario
for asset markets is we have to price we
have to the markets have to acknowledge
that we're going from paradigm A to
paradigm B. And you don't jump to
paradigm B like we talked about a few
weeks ago. You have to go when you're on
the mountain on the top of one mountain.
You don't jump to the top of another
mountain. You go down the mountain
through the valley and up to the next
mountain. And that's that's our core
fundamental belief. And if our core
fundamental beliefs are wrong, then kiss
and Dr. Mo will do what they always do,
which is say, "Hey, Darius, we're better
at investing than you are." Change your
allocations to this. And so, just be
just be confident that Kiss and Dr. Mo,
no matter what I think is going to
happen and what I think is priced in, is
irrelevant to the the the positions that
you're going to have in your portfolio
because we ultimately know that Kiss and
Dr. Mo are much better uh than me at at
predicting where asset markets are
headed and and we're really grateful for
that. So, we'll wrap it up there.
Everyone else, uh, everyone have a
wonderful weekend. We'll catch you back
here next week. Cheers.
